pairs.question: Who is Ehud Gazit and what are his primary academic and professional roles?
pairs.answer: Ehud Gazit is an Israeli biochemist, biophysicist, and nanotechnologist. He serves as a Professor and Endowed Chair at Tel Aviv University and is a member of the university's executive board since 2017. His roles have included Vice President for Research and Development (2008–2012), Chief Scientist of the Israeli Ministry of Science and Technology (2012–2014), and membership on the Israeli National Council for Research and Development (2014–2019).

pairs.question: What prestigious international position was Ehud Gazit elected to in 2023?
pairs.answer: In 2023, Ehud Gazit was elected as the International Solvey Chair in Chemistry, a position previously held by 15 leading scientists, including four Nobel laureates.

pairs.question: For what achievements was Ehud Gazit knighted by the Italian Republic in 2015?
pairs.answer: Ehud Gazit was knighted by the Italian Republic in 2015 for his contributions to science and society.

pairs.question: Describe Ehud Gazit's administrative roles at Tel Aviv University prior to his appointment as Vice President for Research and Development in 2008.
pairs.answer: Before becoming Vice President, Gazit held several academic and administrative positions at Tel Aviv University, including Head of the Chemistry-Biology double major track, member of the University Committee for Appointments and Promotions, Head of the Academic Committee of the Ilona Rich Institute for Nano-Biology and Nano-Biotechnology, and member of the managing board of the Center for Nanoscience and Nanotechnology.

pairs.question: What was Ehud Gazit's role as Chief Scientist of the Israeli Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST)?
pairs.answer: From 2012 to 2014, Ehud Gazit served as the Chief Scientist of the Israeli Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST) and coordinated the forum of Chief Scientists of the Israeli ministries.

pairs.question: How did Ehud Gazit contribute to technology transfer at Tel Aviv University during his tenure as Vice President for Research and Development?
pairs.answer: During his tenure as Vice President for Research and Development (2008–2012), Gazit served as the chairman of the board of directors of Ramot Ltd., Tel Aviv University's technology transfer company.

pairs.question: What is the Israeli National Council for Research and Development (NCRD), and how was Ehud Gazit involved with it?
pairs.answer: The Israeli National Council for Research and Development (NCRD) advises the Israeli government on scientific policy. Ehud Gazit served as a member of the NCRD from 2014 to 2019, appointed by the Israeli president.
